startup_description: Simple invoice creation and payment tracking tool for freelancers. Creates professional PDFs, tracks payment status, sends automated reminders. No complicated accounting features - just fast invoicing.

hero_answers.Q1_problem_customer: Solo freelancers waste 3-5 hours per week manually creating invoices in Word, tracking payments in Excel spreadsheets, and remembering to follow up with late-paying clients. They lose $1000-2000 per month in payments they never chase down because manual follow-up is too time-consuming and they forget.
Q2_target_user: Freelance creative professionals (designers, writers, photographers) billing 5-20 clients per month with invoices ranging $500-5000
Solo consultants with similar project-based billing patterns
Small service providers managing multiple small clients
hero_answers.Q3_success_metric: Within 60 days of launch: 100 user signups, 40 active users who created at least 3 invoices, 8 paying subscribers converting from free trial, 200+ invoices generated total, and $250 MRR proving subscription model works.
Q4_must_have_features: User registration and login - Email/password authentication with email verification
Invoice generator - Simple form to create branded PDF invoices with logo and line items
Client database - Store client contact info and view invoice history per client
Payment tracker - Dashboard showing unpaid, overdue, and paid invoices with status filtering
Auto-reminders - System sends email reminders 7 days after invoice becomes overdue
Q5_non_goals: Stripe payment processing or Pay Now button for clients to pay online
Recurring invoices or subscription billing for clients
Full accounting suite with expense tracking or P&L reports

Q6_constraints.brand_positioning: Fastest way to create and track invoices - no bloat, no complexity
Q6_constraints.scope: Invoice creation and payment tracking only - nothing else in v1
Q6_constraints.economics: Monthly subscription $19-39/month with $1-2/user operational costs gives healthy margins
Q6_constraints.build_time: 90 days total - 60 days to build MVP, 30 days for user testing and bug fixes before public launch
Q6_constraints.tech_requirements: Single-user accounts (NOT multi-tenant), email-based auth, PDF generation, scheduled reminder job

Q7_data_sources: User manual input - All client data, invoice line items, business settings entered by user
System-generated - Invoice numbers (sequential), timestamps, payment status
No external data sources required for MVP
Q8_integrations: SendGrid or AWS SES - Transactional email for invoice delivery and automated payment reminders (required for MVP)
Stripe - Subscription billing for user accounts paying monthly subscription (required for MVP)
Phase 2: QuickBooks API for accounting sync
Phase 2: Stripe Checkout for client-facing Pay Invoice button
Q9_risks: Email deliverability issues if SPF/DKIM not configured properly - users will blame product if invoices don't arrive
PDF generation bugs with formatting or fonts - allocate extra testing time
Background job failures for reminders would break core value prop - need monitoring and alerts
Competition from free tools like Wave and FreshBooks free tier
Users expect mobile app but we're web-only in v1 - may lose some signups
hero_answers.Q10_shipping_preference: Start with core invoice generation, client management, and payment tracking with automated reminders. Build user auth and subscription billing first. No Stripe client payments or accounting integrations until v2 after validating core workflow works.

external_apis: SendGrid (transactional email for invoice delivery and reminders)
Stripe (subscription billing for user monthly payments)
